cards.front: What is the main idea behind Stackelberg's model of duopoly?
cards.back: The Stackelberg model assumes one firm has a strategic advantage over the other, allowing it to make the first move and set the equilibrium price and quantity.

cards.front: What is the optimal strategy for the follower firm in the Stackelberg model?
cards.back: The follower firm should mimic the price and quantity set by the leader firm.

cards.front: What is the outcome of the Stackelberg model, when the follower firm perfectly anticipates the leader firm's move?
cards.back: The follower firm will set a price and quantity that will yield the same profit as the leader firm.

cards.front: What is the name of the leader firm in the Stackelberg model?
cards.back: The leader firm is the firm that makes the first move and sets the equilibrium price and quantity.

cards.front: What is the term for the benefit gained by the leader firm in the Stackelberg model?
cards.back: The leader firm gains a first-mover advantage or deadweight loss due to its ability to set the equilibrium price and quantity.
